lean:
theorem Rudin_exercise_3_6a
: Tendsto (λ (n : ℕ) => (∑ i ∈ range n, g i)) atTop atTop
reasoning: Assuming `g` is defined elsewhere as `g i = √(i+1) − √i` (standard ProofNet convention), the Lean statement asserts that the partial sums `∑ i < n, g i` tend to `+∞` as `n → ∞`, which is exactly the informal claim that `lim_{n→∞} ∑_{i<n} a_i = ∞`. The use of `Tendsto … atTop atTop` is the standard Mathlib encoding of "diverges to +∞", and `Finset.range n` corresponds to `i < n`. No extra hypotheses are added and no quantifier structure is changed.
